description: Wrapper CLI that captures AI conversations and syncs them to a server. Reviewers and agents can query the conversations behind any PR.

userStories.title: Server scaffold: Node.js + Express + Postgres connection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a running Express server connected to Postgres so that subsequent stories can add endpoints and schema on top of it.
acceptanceCriteria: Node.js + TypeScript project initialised under server/ with tsconfig, package.json, and a start script
Express app listens on PORT (default 3000)
Postgres connection via DATABASE_URL env var using pg or postgres npm package
GET /health returns 200 with { status: 'ok' }
Server reads API_KEY env var (used later for auth)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: DB migration: sessions table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a sessions table in Postgres so that the server can persist conversation sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration script at server/migrations/001_sessions.sql (or equivalent)
sessions table has columns: id (text PK), user_name, user_email, working_dir, git_remotes (jsonb), branch (text), tool (text), transcript (text), started_at, updated_at, status (active|done)
Migration runs on server start if table does not exist (simple CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS is fine for POC)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PUT /sessions/:id endpoint
userStories.description: As the CLI, I want to create or update a session on the server so that conversation data is persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /sessions/:id accepts JSON body: { user_name, user_email, working_dir, git_remotes, branch, tool, transcript, status }
Upserts the session row (insert or update on conflict)
Sets updated_at to now() on every upsert
Returns 200 with the saved session object
Requires X-API-Key header matching API_KEY env var — returns 401 if missing or wrong
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GET /sessions and GET /sessions/:id endpoints
userStories.description: As the CLI, I want to list and retrieve sessions so that the data commands can query stored conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /sessions returns array of all sessions, ordered by started_at DESC, excluding transcript field
GET /sessions/:id returns the full session including transcript
GET /sessions accepts optional ?q= query param for case-insensitive text search on transcript
Both endpoints require X-API-Key header
Returns 404 if session not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deploy server to DigitalOcean droplet
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the server running on the DO droplet so that the CLI has a real endpoint to sync to.
acceptanceCriteria: Server is running on the droplet at 24.144.97.81 (SSH key and credentials are in .secrets at repo root)
Postgres is installed and running on the same droplet
Server process is managed by pm2 or similar (auto-restarts on crash)
GET http://24.144.97.81:3000/health returns 200
API_KEY and DATABASE_URL are set as environment variables on the droplet
Deployment steps are documented in server/DEPLOY.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SSH key and droplet IP are in .secrets at repo root. Actual droplet IP is 24.144.97.81 (orchid-deploy).

userStories.title: CLI scaffold: TypeScript project with command routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a CLI binary so that subsequent stories can add commands on top of it.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript project under cli/ with tsconfig, package.json, and build script
Binary entry point at cli/bin/orchid (shebang, built to dist/)
orchid --help prints available commands: claude, data
orchid --version prints the version
Reads ORCHID_API_URL and ORCHID_API_KEY from env (with sensible defaults / error if missing)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid claude: collect git metadata from working directory
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `orchid claude` to collect context about my project so that sessions are linked to the right repos.
acceptanceCriteria: orchid claude <args> subcommand exists
Collects git_remotes: runs `git remote get-url origin` in the working dir and any immediate subdirectories that are git repos
Collects user_name and user_email from `git config user.name` and `git config user.email`
Collects branch from `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` (or 'detached' if not on a branch)
Gracefully handles non-git directories (empty remotes, unknown user)
Prints collected metadata to stderr before launching
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid claude: launch wrapped tool and detect transcript file
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `orchid claude` to launch Claude Code and find its JSONL transcript so that I can sync it.
acceptanceCriteria: Spawns `claude <args>` as a child process, forwarding all stdin/stdout/stderr
After spawning, polls ~/.claude/projects/ every 2s to detect the JSONL file created for this session
Identifies the correct file as the newest .jsonl file created after the process started
Stores the detected transcript path for the sync watcher
Process exits with the same exit code as the child process
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Claude Code writes session transcripts to ~/.claude/projects/<hashed-path>/<session-id>.jsonl

userStories.title: orchid claude: periodic sync watcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the conversation to sync to the server every 5 seconds so that data is never lost even if the session crashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Once transcript file is detected, starts a watcher that reads it and PUTs to /sessions/:id every 5 seconds
Session id is derived from the JSONL filename (strip .jsonl extension)
PUT body includes all collected metadata + full transcript content as a string
Sync is idempotent — re-uploading the same content is safe
Sync errors are logged to stderr but do not crash the watcher
Status is set to 'active' during the session
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid claude: final sync on exit
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a final sync when the session ends so that the last messages are always captured.
acceptanceCriteria: On child process exit (and on SIGTERM/SIGINT), performs one final PUT with the latest transcript content
Sets status to 'done' in the final sync
Waits for the final sync to complete before exiting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid data list command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to list stored sessions so that I can see what conversations are available.
acceptanceCriteria: orchid data list fetches GET /sessions and prints results as a table
Table columns: id (short), user_name, time ago, message count (count newlines in transcript), status
Message count shows as '? messages' if transcript not returned by list endpoint
Each row shows truncated working_dir (basename only)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid data show command
userStories.description: As a developer or agent, I want to dump a full session transcript so that I can read the conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: orchid data show <session_id> fetches GET /sessions/:id and prints the raw transcript to stdout
orchid data show <session_id> --turns parses the JSONL and prints turn-by-turn: role + text content only
orchid data show <session_id> --summary prints: session metadata header + first user message + last assistant message
Returns a clear error message if session not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: orchid data search command
userStories.description: As a developer or agent, I want to search across all conversations so that I can find relevant context.
acceptanceCriteria: orchid data search <query> fetches GET /sessions?q=<query> and prints matching sessions
Output lists matching sessions with: id, user, time, and one line of matching context (substring around first match)
If no results, prints 'No sessions matching <query>'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-end smoke test: capture a session and query it back
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an automated E2E test that proves the full capture→store→query flow works so that we know the POC is solid.
acceptanceCriteria: Test script at cli/test/e2e.ts (or .sh) that: writes a fake JSONL transcript file, calls PUT /sessions/:id directly, calls GET /sessions and verifies the session appears, calls GET /sessions/:id and verifies the transcript is returned, calls GET /sessions?q=<term> and verifies the session is found
Test runs against a real server (uses ORCHID_API_URL env var)
Test exits 0 on success, non-zero on failure
npm run test:e2e (or equivalent) runs the test
Typecheck passes
